name: trt-cloud

metadata.name: trt-cloud
metadata.license: NVIDIA CLOUD AGREEMENT + SERVICE-SPECIFIC TERMS FOR NVIDIA TensorRT CLOUD SERVICE (see LICENSE.txt and PREBUILT_ENGINE_LICENSE.txt)
metadata.summary: A CLI utility for using TensorRT Cloud

documentation:
# TensorRT Cloud CLI

This is a command-line client for TensorRT Cloud.
It is used for building TensorRT engines on NVIDIA's cloud and downloading engines
prebuilt and made publicly available by NVIDIA.

## Documentation.

For documentation, please refer to [TensorRT Cloud Docs](https://docs.nvidia.com/deeplearning/tensorrt-cloud/latest/index.html)

documentation: Adds the 'login' subcommand to the main CLI argument parser.

methods.name: trt_cloud.subcommands.login.LoginSubcommand.run
documentation:
Execute the 'login' subcommand with the given args.

The following usages are valid:
- Specifying both args.client_id and args.client_secret
- Specifying only args.nvapi_key
- Empty args (this triggers an interactive login).

Raises ValueError if args are invalid.

classes.name: trt_cloud.nvcf.NVCFClient

methods.name: trt_cloud.nvcf.NVCFClient.auth_header
documentation: Returns the Authentication header needed to call NVCF.

methods.name: trt_cloud.nvcf.NVCFClient.get_functions
documentation: Get all NVCF functions available to this user..

methods.name: trt_cloud.nvcf.NVCFClient.get_request_status
documentation:
Get details for the NVCF function invocation with the given request ID.
Possible return status:
    200: The function invocation is complete. The response content has the return value
         of the NVCF function.
    202: The function invocation is incomplete.
    302: The function invocation is complete. See the "Location" header in the response for
         the download URL of the result.
    404: Unknown request ID. This will happen if get_request_status is called
         after already having returned status 200 or 302.

methods.name: trt_cloud.nvcf.NVCFClient.get_request_position_in_queue
documentation:
Return the position of request_id in the NVCF function queue.
The return value is either the number converted into a string,
or "Unknown" if the position is unknown.

methods.name: trt_cloud.nvcf.NVCFClient.call_function
documentation:
Call a NVCF function.
See get_request_status for a list of possible response status codes.

methods.name: trt_cloud.nvcf.NVCFClient.upload_new_asset
documentation:
Upload an asset to NVCF.
Returns the Asset ID of the created asset.
Class for listing and invoking NVCF Functions.

documentation: Get the latest versions of available engine-building NVCF functions.

documentation:
Get a list of tags from nvcf_func.tags that
need to check with user inputs

methods.name: trt_cloud.client.TRTCloud.start_onnx_build
documentation:
Build a TRT Engine from an ONNX model on the cloud.

Parameters:
onnx_model: str
    The onnx model to build into a TRT engine. Can either be the path to a local
    file, or a HTTP/HTTPS URL. If the ONNX model uses external weights, this file
    should be a ZIP containing the .onnx model along with the extrernal weight files.
gpu: str
    The GPU model which the engine should be built for. Use `trt-cloud info` to get
    the list of available GPUs.
os_name: str
    The name of the OS which the engine will be used on - "linux" or "windows".
strip_weights: bool
    Strip weights from the ONNX model before uploading it to TRT Cloud. The engine
    returned by the server will be weight-stripped. After the engine is downloaded,
    it will be refit with the original weights if 'local_refit' is True.
trtexec_args: List[str]
    Additional command-line arguments to pass to trtexec when building the engine.
    See the user guide for the list of allowed trtexec arguments.
local_refit: bool
    Used only when 'strip_weights' is True. If 'local_refit' is True, the downloaded engine
    will be refit locally with the original weights in the ONNX model.
trt_version: str
    The TRT Version to build the engine for. Can be "default", "latest", or a
    numerical TRT version such as "10.0".
out_file: str
    File path to which the build result should be saved to.
function_id: str
    If specified, uses this NVCF Function ID
    regardless of specified GPU, OS, and TRT Version.
function_version: str
    If specified, uses this NVCF Function version ID
    regardless of specified GPU, OS, and TRT Version.
tags: List[str]
    List of tags to filter available functions by.

methods.name: trt_cloud.client.TRTCloud.continue_build
documentation:
Poll and download a previously-started engine build. This method is useful when
the client was interrupted when waiting for the build to complete.

Parameters:
request_id: str
    The request ID of the build. The request ID is logged to the console when
    a build is started.
out_file: str
    File path to which the build result should be saved to.

methods.name: trt_cloud.client.TRTCloud._poll_build_until_finished
documentation: Poll a NVCF function status until it returns a status that is not 202.

methods.name: trt_cloud.client.TRTCloud._save_build_result
documentation:
Handle a completed build given a response from NVCF.

Either save it to a file or print out the download URL.

methods.name: trt_cloud.client.TRTCloud._cached_nvcf_asset_upload
documentation:
Check whether the file is already stored in the NVCF Asset Cache.
Otherwise, upload the file as a new NVCF asset.
If the file is larger than allowed by NVCF, split it into multiple assets.

methods.name: trt_cloud.client.TRTCloud._handle_possible_error_response
documentation: If the NVCF response is an error, raise a BuilderFunctionException.

methods.name: trt_cloud.client.TRTCloud.get_prebuilt_models
documentation:
Return the list of Deep Learning model names for which
there are prebuilt engines available on TensorRT Cloud.

methods.name: trt_cloud.client.TRTCloud.get_prebuilt_engines
documentation: Return the list of NVIDIA's prebuilt TensorRT engines available for download.

methods.name: trt_cloud.client.TRTCloud.download_prebuilt_engine
documentation: Download a Prebuilt TRT engine from TensorRT Cloud.
A client for building and downloading TRT Cloud inference engines.
